📊 Statistics Calculator

Calculate mean, median, mode, range and standard deviation of any dataset instantly.

Enter a dataset

When to use each measure

Mean: Best when data is roughly symmetrical with no extreme outliers. Good for test scores, temperatures.

Median: Best when data has outliers or is skewed. Better for house prices, incomes where a few very high values distort the mean.

Mode: Best for categorical data or when you need the most common value. Used in fashion sizes, most popular products.

Standard deviation: Measures how spread out the data is. Low = data clustered near the mean. High = data widely spread.

What is descriptive statistics?

Descriptive statistics summarise and describe the main features of a dataset. The key measures are central tendency (mean, median, mode — what is typical) and dispersion (range, standard deviation — how spread out the data is).

Frequently asked questions

What is standard deviation?

Standard deviation measures how much the values in a dataset vary from the mean. A low standard deviation means values are close to the mean; a high one means they are spread out widely.

Can there be more than one mode?

Yes. If two values appear with equal maximum frequency, both are modes (bimodal). If all values are unique, there is no mode.

What is the difference between population and sample standard deviation?

This calculator uses population standard deviation (divides by n). Sample standard deviation divides by n−1 and is used when your dataset is a sample of a larger population.
